name: Umm ul-Banin
title: Mother of Sacrifice
shortDescription: The devoted wife of Imam Ali (AS) and mother of Abbas who, although not in Karbala, kept the history of Ashura alive with her mourning.
fullStory:
Fatima al-Kilabiyya, known as Umm ul-Banin, was Amir al-Mu'minin's (AS) wife after the martyrdom of Lady Fatima (AS). She was a noble and brave woman who bore four valiant sons for Imam Ali (AS): Abbas, Abdullah, Uthman, and Ja'far.

Although Umm ul-Banin stayed in Medina due to old age or illness and was not present in Karbala, she taught her children the lesson of sacrifice and courtesy so well that all four became martyrs for their brother Imam Hussain (AS) on Ashura.

When the captives' caravan returned to Medina and Bashir brought the news of her sons' martyrdom, Umm ul-Banin said: 'My children and everything under the sky be sacrificed for Hussain! Tell me about Hussain.'

Every day she went to Baqi' and recited heart-wrenching elegies for Abbas and his brothers that made even enemies like Marwan ibn al-Hakam cry. She was the founder of traditional and lasting Ashura mourning in Medina.
quoteArabic: أَوْلادِي وَمَنْ تَحْتَ الخَضْراءِ كُلُّهُمْ فِداءٌ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الحُسَيْنِ...
quote: (Her amazing loyalty:) My children and everything under the sky are sacrificed for Aba Abdullah al-Hussain; tell me about Hussain!
